title: Delissea submuricata
intro: Delissea submuricata, commonly known as the 'Hawaiian mountain hibiscus', is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands. This plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Delissea submuricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Delissea submuricata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Hawaiian remedies, such as a cough syrup and a poultice for skin irritations.
